item.title: Graduate Panel Data Econometrics
item.description:
General Info

Discussion section: Tuesday from 5:00 to 6:30 pm, SSB 107. Office hour: Tuesday from 6:30 to 7:00 pm, SSB 107.

Material

  1. Review of causal inference and average partial effects. ( )
  2. From Pooled OLS to FE model : unbiasedness, consistency, efficiency, random effect vs fixed effect, IV and FE model. ( )
  3. Two exercises on Two-way Fixed Effect regression. ( )
  4. From the basic Difference-in-differences estimator to Staggered DID in potential outcomes and Callaway and Sant’Anna (2021) approach. ( )
  5. Practice midterm : static and dynamic panel data. ( )
  6. M-Estimation : general consistency theorem, uniform law of large numbers, application to a binary probability model. ( )
  7. Probit model : probit model vs. linear projection, MLE estimation and structural identification with external instrument. ( )
  8. Practice final : binary outcomes, average partial effect, multinomial logir and independence from irrelavent alternatives. ( )

Evaluation

Instructional Assistant Student Evaluation of Teaching .

item.title: Graduate Cross Section Econometrics
item.description:
General Info

Discussion Section: Thursday from 5:00 to 6:30 pm, SSB 107. Office hour: Thursday from 6:30 to 7:00 pm, SSB 107.

Material

  1. Basics of asymptotic theory: convergence theorems, asymptotic linear representation and delta method. Exercises on asymptotic of ratio of two estimators and Hodge’s estimator. ( )
  2. Introduction to potential outcomes and some linear regression arithmetic: orthogonal projection, coefficient of determination and Frisch–Waugh–Lovell Theorem. ( )
  3. Where is the OLS estimator converging to? Exogeneity vs uncorrelatedness vs linear projection. Introduction to bayesian inference and exercise on ridge regression. ( )
  4. Midterm review: Q1 algebraic properties of OLS; Q2 asymptotic properties of empirical CDF estimator. ( )
  5. Selection on observables. Inverse probability weighting, regression adjustment estimator and an exercise on propensity score and fat-tailed distributions. ( )
  6. Practice final: asymptotics of moving average of order one, CLT for dependent data. ( )
  7. IV estimators and LATE: comparison MoM, Wald, TSLS and control function approach, LATE in Threshold Crossing Model. ( )
  8. M-Estimation: general consistency theorem and uniform law of large numbers. Exercise on the estimation of a right-censored Poisson Survival model. ( )

Evaluation

Instructional Assistant Student Evaluation of Teaching .
